task: I need to get ready for a school project this week, and I’m trying to keep track of what to do first, what to bring, and what can wait until later. Can you help me organize it in a way that would be easy to use while I’m working?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, easy-to-use way to organize it while you work:

## School Project Plan

### 1) Do First
- Read the project directions
- Check the due date
- Make sure you know what needs to be turned in
- Gather any important papers or notes

### 2) Bring / Have Ready
- Project instructions
- Notebook or paper
- Pencil or pen
- Folder or binder
- Any materials your teacher asked for
- Device or charger, if needed

### 3) Do Next
- Start the first part of the project
- Work on the main assignment
- Check your work as you go
- Put finished parts in one place

### 4) Can Wait Until Later
- Final decorations
- Extra details
- Neat copying or rewriting
- Double-checking everything at the end

### 5) Quick Check Before You Stop
- Is the important part done?
- Did I save or keep my work together?
- Do I know what to do next time?
- Is everything packed back up?

If you want, I can turn this into a **short checklist** or a **day-by-day plan for this week**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this is fine, but I want it even more usable. Make it like a real checklist I can keep beside me, with boxes and maybe a simple order for the week. The one you gave is okay, just a little too general. Also, if the project needs stuff to bring on different days, split that out so it’s not all mixed together.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a more usable **week checklist** you can keep beside you. I split the **bring items** by day so it’s easier to follow.

## School Project Week Checklist

### Monday
- [ ] Read the project directions
- [ ] Check the due date
- [ ] Write down what needs to be finished
- [ ] Gather the first materials I need

**Bring:**
- [ ] Project directions
- [ ] Notebook or paper
- [ ] Pencil/pen

### Tuesday
- [ ] Start the project
- [ ] Do the first main part
- [ ] Check that I’m doing it the right way

**Bring:**
- [ ] Materials for the first part
- [ ] Folder or binder
- [ ] Pencil/pen

### Wednesday
- [ ] Keep working on the project
- [ ] Finish the next part
- [ ] Put completed work together in one place

**Bring:**
- [ ] Materials I still need
- [ ] Any notes or handouts
- [ ] Pencil/pen

### Thursday
- [ ] Finish most of the project
- [ ] Add details that are important
- [ ] Check for anything missing

**Bring:**
- [ ] Project pieces
- [ ] Glue, scissors, markers, or other supplies
- [ ] Pencil/pen

### Friday
- [ ] Do the final check
- [ ] Make sure everything is complete
- [ ] Pack the project to turn in
- [ ] Put my name on it

**Bring:**
- [ ] Finished project
- [ ] Any last papers or parts
- [ ] Folder or bag to carry it

## Can Wait Until Later
- [ ] Extra decorations
- [ ] Fancy neat copies
- [ ] Small details that are not required
- [ ] Final touch-ups at the end

## Quick Stop Check
- [ ] Do I know what to do next?
- [ ] Is my work together?
- [ ] Did I bring everything I need for tomorrow?
- [ ] Is anything important still missing?

If you want, I can also turn this into a **morning / after-school / before-bed checklist** for the whole week.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this works. The day-by-day checklist is what I wanted, and splitting the stuff to bring by day makes it easier to use. Nothing extra needed.
